lines.en_lit: Come hear as Rabba bar Shmuel taught a pregnant woman who smells one feeds her until she is satisfied, and one whom a mad dog bit, one feeds him from the lobe of its liver; and one who has pain in his mouth, one places medicine in his on Shabbat; the statement of Rabbi Elazar, son of Rabbi Yosei, who said in the name of Rabbi Matya ben Ḥarash. And the Rabbis say: In this case and no other. To which this one If we say a pregnant woman, it obvious; is there anyone who says not a pregnant woman Rather, is it not medicine Learn from this

Rav Ashi said: the mishna is also precise And furthermore, Rabbi Matya ben Ḥarash said: one who suffers pain in his mouth, one places medicine in his on Shabbat, and the Rabbis do not disagree And if it is so that the Rabbis disagree with him, let combine and teach them and let the Rabbis disagree in the latter clause. learn from here

because uncertainty life-threatening Why do I furthermore: And any uncertainty life-threatening overrides Shabbat? Rav Yehuda said Rav said: They stated not only uncertainty this Shabbat, but even uncertainty a different Shabbat.

What are the circumstances where assess for eight days, and the first day is Shabbat. Lest you say: He should wait until evening so they will not need to desecrate two Shabbatot for his sake, it teaches us

That was also taught One heats water for an ill person on Shabbat, whether to give him to drink or to wash him, And they did not say only current Shabbat but even a different, Shabbat. And one must not say: Let us wait for him perhaps he will get well Rather, one heats it for him immediately because any uncertainty life-threatening overrides Shabbat. And not uncertainty on the current Shabbat, but even uncertainty on a different Shabbat.

And these acts should not be performed by gentiles or Samaritans but by the greatest of the Jewish people, And one does not say: These actions be performed of women or Samaritans, However, these do combine with an additional opinion,

The Sages taught One engages saving a life on Shabbat, and one who is vigilant is praiseworthy. And one need not take permission from a court How so? one sees a child who fell into the sea, he spreads net and raises him And one who is vigilant is praiseworthy, and one need not seek permission from a court, although he catches fish one sees a child fall into a pit he digs part of the ground out and raises him out. And one who is vigilant is praiseworthy, and one need not seek permission from a court, although in doing so he fashions a step.

one sees that a door is locked before a child he breaks the door and takes the child out. And one who is vigilant is praiseworthy, and one need not seek permission from a court, although he intends to break into boards one extinguish a fire placing a barrier in front of on Shabbat And one who is vigilant is praiseworthy, and one need not seek permission from a court, although he leaves the coals,
And necessary As, had it taught us the sea, because in the meantime, will be swept away but a pit, who remains say No, necessary
And if it had taught us the pit, because the child is scared but when a door is locked it is possible to sit on the other side and amuse him with nuts necessary
one extinguish a fire placing a barrier Why do I this? even toward another courtyard.

Rav Yosef said Rav Yehuda said Shmuel said: saving a life, did not follow the majority What are the circumstances? If we say there are nine Jews and one gentile among them the majority Jews half Jews and half gentiles, lenient uncertainty life-threatening
Rather, there are nine gentiles and one Jew. this too obvious. fixed and whenever a fixed it is considered half half

No, necessary separated to another courtyard, Lest you say: whatever is separated is considered to have left from the majority, it teaches us that with regard to saving a life, one does not follow the majority.

Is that so? But didn’t Rav Asi say Rabbi Yoḥanan said: nine gentiles and one Jew in that same courtyard one removes the debris, in another courtyard one does not remove the debris? not difficult; This when they all left That when a minority of them left

Did Shmuel say this, Didn’t we learn one finds an abandoned child if the majority are gentiles a gentile; and if the majority are Jews Jew; half and half Jew? And Rav said: They taught this, only to sustaining him but not to attributing a lineage to him.
